# UX & Checkout Flow Specification Quality Checklist: Cash on Delivery (COD) Checkout Integration

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of user experience and checkout flow requirements before proceeding with implementation.
**Created**: 2026-07-04
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## UX & Configuration Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 - Are the UI layout, position, and styling tokens of the Cash on Delivery toggle and the conditional "Pay Delivery Charge First" sub-toggle defined? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002, Invariant 9]
- [ ] CHK002 - Are design system components and tokens specified for the "COD" badge on dashboard order list and detail screens? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Invariant 9]
- [ ] CHK003 - Are the exact wording and localization requirements for the storefront COD payment option label and description defined? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK004 - Are standard system loading states and error messages specified for when a merchant updates or saves Payments configuration? [Completeness, Gap]
- [ ] CHK005 - Does the spec define whether a merchant is notified on the dashboard when a plan downgrade occurs and their COD configuration is deactivated? [Completeness, Gap]

## Checkout Flow and Input Validation Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 - Is the phrase "valid transaction ID" quantified with specific format, length, or character-set rules? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK007 - Are the content and format requirements for the bKash/Nagad wallet numbers display instructions clearly specified? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK008 - Is the exact text of the delivery note shown to customers selecting standard COD specified? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK009 - Are the rules for dynamic transition and layout adjustments (e.g., height transitions, input focusing) when switching between payment methods specified? [Clarity, Gap]
- [ ] CHK010 - Is the format and wording of the checkout error notification specified if the order submission fails due to out-of-stock products? [Clarity, Gap]

## Edge Case and Exception Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK011 - Are storefront checkout validation requirements defined for the case where a merchant disables COD while a customer has it selected on the checkout screen? [Coverage, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK012 - Does the spec define whether a customer can edit or resubmit their Transaction ID if the merchant rejects the upfront delivery charge transaction? [Coverage, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK013 - Are user interface requirements specified for the order detail page when an order is in a `returned` status? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK014 - Are checkout recovery flows specified if a payment API timeout or error occurs during transaction ID validation? [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK015 - Is the behavior of the checkout page specified if the customer's cart contains items that went out of stock during the checkout session? [Coverage, Edge Cases]

## Measurability & Consistency of UX Requirements

- [ ] CHK016 - Can the success outcome "checkout completion step under 15 seconds" be objectively measured using a specified protocol? [Measurability, SC-001]
- [ ] CHK017 - Do the storefront payment option list requirements align consistently with the checkout schemas defined in the data model? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Data Model §2]
- [ ] CHK018 - Is the rule that prevents merchants on ineligible plans from enabling COD consistent between the settings UI definition and the server-side action? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Invariant 7]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
